description: My thoughts and observations about everything and anything

trongate.intro:
Trongate is an exciting PHP framework that challenges the established standards and offers a simple yet effective approach to development.
Its strength lies in its simplicity, which can be refreshing at a time when complexity is often seen as a necessity.
trongate.description_x:
Trongate is a PHP framework that goes against the grain of modern web development.
Where many other frameworks focus on including a multitude of features and complex tools, Trongate chooses a more minimalistic and straightforward approach.
It breaks with the established standards, not by ignoring them, but by simplifying them to their core.
This framework stands out by emphasizing simplicity and efficiency.
Instead of offering an extensive library of features, it focuses on the most essential elements that make it easy for developers to build applications without unnecessary complexity.
This approach can be a breath of fresh air for developers who are tired of the steep learning curve and heavy workarounds that often come with other frameworks.
Trongate is not necessarily for those looking for a wealth of built-in options and advanced features.
Rather, it is for those who appreciate the 'less is more' principle.
The framework offers a robust yet lightweight framework where simplicity does not compromise quality or security.
It can be seen as a return to the basics of web development, where code is written to solve problems, not to adapt to an abundance of features.
However, this simplistic approach does not mean that Trongate is limited.
Rather, it gives developers the freedom to expand and adapt as needed without being hampered by an overwhelming structure.
The beauty of Trongate lies in its ability to provide a solid and reliable platform, while allowing room for creativity and individuality in the development process.
trongate.summary:
Trongate is often misunderstood and given a bad reputation because it breaks with the usual standards and takes a journey back to the roots.
In this feature, I will explore and highlight this diamond in the rough that deserves a place in the spotlight.

favourites.headline: Trongate brings many exciting features, here are my favourites:

mhavc.headline: Modular hierarchical asset view controller architecture
mhavc.description:
One of the coolest things about Trongate is the architecture that it all builds on and assumes.
It makes it easy to quickly see from the URL which module is being called and at the same time modules can easily call each other through the Trongate base class.

trongate.mhavc:
This is where Trongate shines, you get a single starting point with a solid starting architecture due to the fact that everything is divided into modules, incl. 
media and JavaScript files.
trongate.missing_i18n:
One criticism that could be given is that internationalization (i18n) is not thought of and there is actually no further support for languages.
That is correct and something I previously have dealt with, it is in the plans and I look forward to seeing how it is resolved. :)
trongate.callback_validation: Another feature that often flies under the radar is <u>callback</u> based validation
trongate.hope: My hope with this article is to spark people's interest and highlight a diamond in the rough that has unfortunately gotten a bit of a bad reputation.
